source_jobs.title: AI Agent Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hello Patient is hiring a technical, high-agency AI Agent Product Manager to own the end-to-end delivery of AI agents in real customer environments. We build AI-powered voice, sms, and chat agents that integrate deeply into how healthcare practices actually operate.

Every new customer deployment is a product launch—new workflows, new edge cases, new integration requirements, new ways the agent needs to behave in the real world. This role exists to own that launch from scoping through go-live. You’ll also own the customer-facing roadmap for each deployment—defining requirements, sequencing what gets built, and aligning stakeholders on tradeoffs.

This role combines product ownership with hands-on agent building — translating requirements into shipped agent behavior. You’ll gather requirements directly from customers, translate them into agent logic and workflows, and implement that behavior hands-on using prompts, guardrails, tools, and evals.

You’ll design Mia’s behavior, ship production-ready multi-agent workflows, and turn real-world healthcare complexity into systems that actually hold up on live calls. This is a rare seat: you’ll get direct exposure to frontier LLM failure modes in production, and you’ll continuously iterate on agent performance using structured evaluation, testing, and real customer feedback.



WHAT YOU'LL DO


OWN CUSTOMER DEPLOYMENTS END-TO-END

 - Lead delivery of net-new AI agent deployments as the single accountable owner—from customer requirements through production go-live

 - Treat every deployment as a product launch: define scope, manage tradeoffs, hit timelines, ensure quality

 - Own the customer-facing roadmap for each deployment—what are we shipping, in what order, and why


BUILD AGENTS, NOT JUST SPECS

 - Gather requirements directly from customers and translate them into agent behavior yourself using our internal platform—prompts, tools, guardrails, multi-agent workflows

 - Do hands-on prompt engineering using AI tools to build the components that make agents work: conversation flows, decision logic, edge case handling, integration behaviors

 - Ship production-ready agents that handle real healthcare complexity: scheduling logic, patient context, EMR data, practice-specific edge cases

 - Continuously improve agent performance using structured evals, live call review, and direct customer feedback


DRIVE ZERO-TO-ONE EXECUTION

 - Lead deployments for new workflows, integrations, and verticals where no playbook exists

 - Identify unknowns early, derisk them, and make tradeoff decisions that keep launches on track

 - When deployments require net-new platform capabilities, own requirements and prioritization with the engineering team


WORK ALONGSIDE CONVERSATIONAL DESIGN AND CUSTOMER ENGINEERING

 - Conversational designers design and optimize agent behavior—they review real conversations, identify patterns, design conversation flows, and ship agent behavior

 - Customer engineers build integrations into our customers' systems of record—EHRs, PMS, and PIMS

 - You own everything else: integration scoping with Customer Engineering, cross-team coordination, timeline management, stakeholder communication, and unblocking whatever's in the way


BUILD TOWARD REPEATABILITY

 - Identify patterns across launches and codify them into playbooks

 - Know when a workflow or integration is ready to transition from zero-to-one ownership to steady-state implementation

 - Hand off proven patterns to implementation teams and move on to the next net-new challenge


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST HAVE

 - 1+ years in Product Management or a closely related role (e.g., technical program management at a product-led company, founder/early employee wearing the PM hat) or a technical background (software engineer / forward-deployed engineer) who has operated in a PM-like capacity

 - Strong technical product background—comfortable with APIs, integrations, and understanding how systems connec

 - Strong technical product background—comfortable with APIs, integrations, and understanding how systems connec

source_jobs.title: Full-Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Role Overview

Full-Stack Software Engineer

As a Full‑Stack Software Engineer at Hello Patient, you’ll design, build, and scale the end‑to‑end systems that power Mia, our AI assistant for healthcare practices. From real‑time voice and messaging services to EHR integrations and admin dashboards, you’ll ship production‑ready code across the stack, own features from concept to deployment, and help set engineering best practices in a hyper‑growth, compliance‑critical environment.

Location: Austin, TX or NYC

Reports to: CTO

Type: Full‑time, exempt

Key Responsibilities

 - Feature Development. Deliver user‑facing functionality in NextJS/TypeScript while crafting robust, testable APIs in Python/FastAPI. Translate product specs into end‑to‑end experiences that delight clinic staff, patients, and our implementation team.

 - System Architecture & Scalability. Design and optimize backend systems that support high-volume, low-latency voice, SMS, and chat traffic. Optimize for reliability, horizontal scaling, and efficient token usage with LLM back‑ends.

 - Data & Integrations. Build secure integrations with EHR/PM systems via FHIR, HL7, and custom APIs. Implement data pipelines and storage solutions (PostgreSQL, Redis, S3, and more) that respect HIPAA and SOC‑2 controls.

 - DevOps & CI/CD. Own your code in production: write infrastructure‑as‑code in Terraform and Helm, automate tests, and monitor metrics. Champion a culture of frequent, reliable production deployments through robust CI/CD pipelines.

 - Quality & Security. Write unit, integration, and end‑to‑end tests; conduct code reviews to uphold performance, readability, and security standards. Collaborate with compliance teams to embed encryption, logging, and auditability from day one.

 - Collaboration & Mentorship. Work closely with internal teams to iterate quickly and prioritize user impact. Share knowledge via design docs; mentor junior engineers as the team scales.

Qualifications

Must‑Haves

 - 4+ yrs professional experience building and shipping full‑stack web applications.

 - Proficiency with modern TypeScript on client (React, Next.js) and Python on server

 - AI-native: You use AI tools daily to architect, build, and debug fullstack features, moving quickly from idea to production with minimal hand-holding. You automate grunt work, document as you go, and ship clean, scalable code fast.

 - Strong grasp of relational databases and REST API design; comfortable optimizing queries and indexing strategies.

 - Hands‑on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).

 - Solid understanding of testing strategies, CI/CD workflows, and Git‑based collaboration.

 - Clear communication skills and a bias for action.

Nice‑to‑Haves

 - Exposure to healthcare data standards (FHIR, HL7) or other high‑compliance domains.

 - Experience integrating/fine‑tuning LLMs, building RAG pipelines, designing agent architecture, voice agents

 - Familiarity with event-driven architecture or real-time streaming.

 - Prior work at an early‑stage startup where you wore multiple hats and influenced product direction.

Note that compensation will vary greatly based on level and years of experience.

Why You’ll Love Working Here

 - Real Impact – Help healthcare teams cut hours of admin work and focus more on patient care.

 - Early-Stage Ownership – Join a well-funded startup where you’ll own meaningful projects from day one.

 - Agency to Drive Outcomes – You’ll have the freedom to make decisions, move fast, and see your work directly shape results.

 - Collaborative Team Environment – Work alongside founders who’ve scaled healthcare products and infrastructure nationally.

 - Values-Driven Culture – We operate with empathy, trust, and curiosity in every decision.

 - Great Benefits – Competitive pay, equity, PTO, and comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance.

 - Fast Growth – Build category-defining technology and grow your skills q

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About The Role

Hello Patient is seeking an Enterprise Account Executive to accelerate new customer acquisition across healthcare organizations nationwide. You’ll own the full sales cycle end-to-end—prospecting, discovery, demos, negotiation, and close—while navigating complex stakeholder groups and building trusted relationships with operators and executive decision-makers.

You’ll sell Mia, our AI-powered communication platform that automates high-volume patient conversations across phone, SMS, and chat. You’ll help prospects understand the operational and financial impact of AI automation—reducing administrative burden, improving patient experience, and capturing revenue that would otherwise be missed.

This is a high-ownership role in a fast-moving, early-stage environment. You’ll partner closely with leadership to strengthen messaging, ensure a seamless customer experience, and help scale a repeatable go-to-market motion as we grow. We’re looking for a true hunter who is comfortable creating momentum, staying disciplined in a dynamic environment, and helping shape how we sell as we scale.


What You'll Do



OWN AND DRIVE COMPLEX SALES CYCLES

 - Own the full sales cycle from prospecting through close.

 - Lead structured discovery, tailored demos, pricing discussions, and negotiations.

 - Navigate multi-stakeholder buying groups across operations, finance, and executive leadership.


HUNT STRATEGICALLY INTO ENTERPRISE ACCOUNTS

 - Build and execute outbound strategies to break into target healthcare organizations nationwide.

 - Develop account penetration plans for multi-location groups.

 - Generate pipeline through a combination of outbound, referrals, and strategic prospecting.

 - Move deals from pilot entry points to large-scale enterprise expansions.


DRIVE REVENUE GROWTH & EXPANSION

 - Identify expansion opportunities within existing accounts.

 - Build trusted relationships that unlock long-term growth.

 - Manage pipeline and forecasting in HubSpot with discipline and accuracy.

 - Consistently exceed revenue goals in a commission-driven environment.



HELP SHAPE THE GTM MOTION

 - Partner closely with Sales Leadership, Product, and Implementation.

 - Share customer feedback and competitive insights to influence positioning and roadmap.

 - Contribute to building repeatable sales processes as we scale.

 - Operate with autonomy and adaptability in an evolving startup environment.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST HAVE

 - 3+ years of full-cycle B2B SaaS closing experience.

 - Experience selling mid-market or enterprise deals (ideally $60K+ ACV).

 - Proven track record of consistently hitting or exceeding quota.

 - Strong hunter mentality — comfortable generating your own pipeline.

 - Drive accounts from initial pilot engagements to long-term, enterprise-wide expansion.

 - Experience navigating multi-threaded sales cycles.

 - High ownership, self-direction, and comfort in early-stage environments.

 - Strong consultative discovery and demo skills.

Preferred

 - Experience selling into healthcare organizations.

 - Experience selling AI, automation, or workflow software.

 - Background at high-performing SaaS companies (mid-market or enterprise).

 - Experience selling nationally (not limited to a regional territory model).

 - Startup experience building sales motion from the ground up.


COMPENSATION

 - On-Target Earnings (OTE): $250,000 – $300,000 (uncapped)
   
   - Base salary: $100,000 – $150,000

 - Equity: Meaningful ownership in a fast-growing healthtech startup

About Hello Patient

Hello Patient is transforming healthcare communication through advanced conversational AI. Our founding team spent 3.5 years scaling Carbon Health's technology during hypergrowth, where we saw firsthand how much clinical time gets wasted on basic communication—great clinicians buried in admin work, patients stuck in phone tag.

Our AI platform proactively engages patients through perfectly timed calls and texts,

source_jobs.title: Customer Integration Engineer (Contractor)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hello Patient is building Mia, an AI assistant that transforms how healthcare providers engage with patients. We're hiring a Customer Integration Engineer — you'll be the technical backbone connecting Mia to our customers' existing systems. You'll own the full integration lifecycle — from initial discovery with customer IT teams to building production-grade connectors to ongoing support.

This is an impactful technical role where you'll write code daily and work directly with healthcare customers to solve their unique challenges. You'll collaborate closely with our agent platform team to design and implement new integration approaches as we scale. Client presence matters here — you'll be the primary technical contact for customers, so strong communication skills are just as important as strong code.

This role is based in Mexico City, where you'll work in-person with our local team three days a week. This is an exciting opportunity for an integration engineer who thrives on variety, ownership, and real customer impact!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Build & Ship Integrations

 - Develop production-quality integrations connecting Mia to EHR systems, practice management software, and data warehouses

 - Build robust data pipelines using APIs, middleware, and various data exchange methods

 - Create reusable integration modules to accelerate future implementations

 - Handle technical configuration for new customers and resolve conflicts during deployment

Own the Customer Relationship

 - Lead technical discovery sessions with customer engineering and IT teams

 - Serve as the primary technical contact for integration questions and incident resolution

 - Translate clinical and business workflows into clear technical specs

Support, Document & Improve

 - Provide Tier 2/3 support for production environments

 - Create and maintain technical documentation, integration playbooks, and training materials

 - Identify opportunities to improve and automate integration processes over time



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must Have

 - 5+ years of experience in software development, data engineering, or integration work in production environments

 - Proven track record building and maintaining real integrations — data pipelines, API integrations, or similar

 - Python experience

 - Experience with APIs (REST/SOAP), webhooks, and authentication standards (OAuth, JWT)

 - Strong English communication skills — this hire must be comfortable leading technical conversations with clients and representing Hello Patient professionally

 - Ability to work independently, manage multiple projects, and thrive in a fast-paced startup environment

 - Located in or able to commute to Mexico City (Hybrid – 3 days/week in office)

Preferred

 - Familiarity with healthcare data standards (FHIR, HL7) or EHR integrations

 - Background at a B2B SaaS or startup

 - Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) or containerization (Docker)

 - AI-native mindset — comfortable building around AI tools and platforms



ABOUT HELLO PATIENT

Hello Patient is transforming healthcare communication through advanced conversational AI. Our founding team spent 3.5 years scaling Carbon Health's technology during hypergrowth, where we saw firsthand how much clinical time gets wasted on basic communication—great clinicians buried in admin work, patients stuck in phone tag.

Our AI platform proactively engages patients through perfectly timed calls and texts, resolving complex interactions end-to-end—from scheduling to billing to retention. Built with security and compliance at its core, our technology integrates with existing healthcare infrastructure to deliver 24/7 patient engagement.

Backed by Scale and 8VC, we're building the future of healthcare communication, one conversation at a time.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Build & Ship Integrations

 - Develop production-quality integrations connecting Mia to EHR systems, practice management software, and data warehouses

 - Build robust data pipelines using APIs, middleware, and various data exchange methods

 - Create reusable integration modules to accelerate future implementations

 - Handle technical configuration for new customers and resolve conflicts during deployment

Own the Customer Relationship

 - Lead technical discovery sessions with customer engineering and IT teams

 - Serve as the primary technical contact for integration questions and incident resolution

 - Translate clinical and business workflows into clear technical specs

Support, Document & Improve

 - Provide Tier 2/3 support for production environments

 - Create and maintain technical documentation, integration playbooks, and training materials

 - Identify opportunities to improve and automate integration processes over time



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must Have

 - 5+ years of experience in software development, data engineering, or integration work in production environments

 - Proven track record building and maintaining real integrations — data pipelines, API integrations, or similar

 - Python experience

 - Experience with APIs (REST/SOAP), webhooks, and authentication standards (OAuth, JWT)

 - Strong communication skills — this hire must be comfortable leading technical conversations with clients and representing Hello Patient professionally

 - Ability to work independently, manage multiple projects, and thrive in a fast-paced startup environment

Preferred

 - Familiarity with healthcare data standards (FHIR, HL7) or EHR integrations

 - Background at a B2B SaaS or startup

 - Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) or containerization (Docker)

 - AI-native mindset — comfortable building around AI tools and platforms



COMPENSATION

Base salary: $110,000 – $160,000 USD annually, depending on experience.



ABOUT HELLO PATIENT

Hello Patient is transforming healthcare communication through advanced conversational AI. Our founding team spent 3.5 years scaling Carbon Health's technology during hypergrowth, where we saw firsthand how much clinical time gets wasted on basic communication—great clinicians buried in admin work, patients stuck in phone tag.

Our AI platform proactively engages patients through perfectly timed calls and texts, resolving complex interactions end-to-end—from scheduling to billing to retention. Built with security and compliance at its core, our technology integrates with existing healthcare infrastructure to deliver 24/7 patient engagement.

Backed by Scale and 8VC, we're building the future of healthcare communication, one conversation at a time.

source_jobs.title: Customer Integration Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hello Patient is building Mia, an AI assistant that transforms how healthcare providers engage with patients. We're hiring a Customer Integration Engineer — you'll be the technical backbone connecting Mia to our customers' existing systems. You'll own the full integration lifecycle — from initial discovery with customer IT teams to building production-grade connectors to ongoing support.

This is an impactful technical role where you'll write code daily and work directly with healthcare customers to solve their unique challenges. You'll collaborate closely with our agent platform team to design and implement new integration approaches as we scale. Client presence matters here — you'll be the primary technical contact for customers, so strong communication skills are just as important as strong code.

This is an exciting opportunity for an integration engineer who thrives on variety, ownership, and real customer impact!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Build & Ship Integrations

 - Develop production-quality integrations connecting Mia to EHR systems, practice management software, and data warehouses

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About The Role

Hello Patient is seeking an Enterprise Account Executive to accelerate new customer acquisition across healthcare organizations nationwide. You’ll own the full sales cycle end-to-end—prospecting, discovery, demos, negotiation, and close—while navigating complex stakeholder groups and building trusted relationships with operators and executive decision-makers.

You’ll sell Mia, our AI-powered communication platform that automates high-volume patient conversations across phone, SMS, and chat. You’ll help prospects understand the operational and financial impact of AI automation—reducing administrative burden, improving patient experience, and capturing revenue that would otherwise be missed.

This is a high-ownership role in a fast-moving, early-stage environment. You’ll partner closely with leadership to strengthen messaging, ensure a seamless customer experience, and help scale a repeatable go-to-market motion as we grow. We’re looking for a true hunter who is comfortable creating momentum, staying disciplined in a dynamic environment, and helping shape how we sell as we scale.


What You'll Do



OWN AND DRIVE COMPLEX SALES CYCLES

 - Own the full sales cycle from prospecting through close.

 - Lead structured discovery, tailored demos, pricing discussions, and negotiations.

 - Navigate multi-stakeholder buying groups across operations, finance, and executive leadership.


HUNT STRATEGICALLY INTO ENTERPRISE ACCOUNTS

 - Build and execute outbound strategies to break into target healthcare organizations nationwide.

 - Develop account penetration plans for multi-location groups.

 - Generate pipeline through a combination of outbound, referrals, and strategic prospecting.

 - Move deals from pilot entry points to large-scale enterprise expansions.


DRIVE REVENUE GROWTH & EXPANSION

 - Identify expansion opportunities within existing accounts.

 - Build trusted relationships that unlock long-term growth.

 - Manage pipeline and forecasting in HubSpot with discipline and accuracy.

 - Consistently exceed revenue goals in a commission-driven environment.



HELP SHAPE THE GTM MOTION

 - Partner closely with Sales Leadership, Product, and Implementation.

 - Share customer feedback and competitive insights to influence positioning and roadmap.

 - Contribute to building repeatable sales processes as we scale.

 - Operate with autonomy and adaptability in an evolving startup environment.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST HAVE

 - 3+ years of full-cycle B2B SaaS closing experience.

 - Experience selling mid-market or enterprise deals (ideally $60K+ ACV).

 - Proven track record of consistently hitting or exceeding quota.

 - Strong hunter mentality — comfortable generating your own pipeline.

 - Drive accounts from initial pilot engagements to long-term, enterprise-wide expansion.

 - Experience navigating multi-threaded sales cycles.

 - High ownership, self-direction, and comfort in early-stage environments.

 - Strong consultative discovery and demo skills.

Preferred

 - Experience selling into healthcare organizations.

 - Experience selling AI, automation, or workflow software.

 - Background at high-performing SaaS companies (mid-market or enterprise).

 - Experience selling nationally (not limited to a regional territory model).

 - Startup experience building sales motion from the ground up.


COMPENSATION

 - On-Target Earnings (OTE): $250,000 – $300,000 (uncapped)
   
   - Base salary: $100,000 – $150,000

 - Equity: Meaningful ownership in a fast-growing healthtech startup

About Hello Patient

Hello Patient is transforming healthcare communication through advanced conversational AI. Our founding team spent 3.5 years scaling Carbon Health's technology during hypergrowth, where we saw firsthand how much clinical time gets wasted on basic communication—great clinicians buried in admin work, patients stuck in phone tag.

Our AI platform proactively engages patients through perfectly timed calls and texts,

source_jobs.title: AI Agent Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hello Patient is hiring a technical, high-agency AI Agent Product Manager to own the end-to-end delivery of AI agents in real customer environments. We build AI-powered voice, sms, and chat agents that integrate deeply into how healthcare practices actually operate.

Every new customer deployment is a product launch—new workflows, new edge cases, new integration requirements, new ways the agent needs to behave in the real world. This role exists to own that launch from scoping through go-live. You’ll also own the customer-facing roadmap for each deployment—defining requirements, sequencing what gets built, and aligning stakeholders on tradeoffs.

This role combines product ownership with hands-on agent building — translating requirements into shipped agent behavior. You’ll gather requirements directly from customers, translate them into agent logic and workflows, and implement that behavior hands-on using prompts, guardrails, tools, and evals.

You’ll design Mia’s behavior, ship production-ready multi-agent workflows, and turn real-world healthcare complexity into systems that actually hold up on live calls. This is a rare seat: you’ll get direct exposure to frontier LLM failure modes in production, and you’ll continuously iterate on agent performance using structured evaluation, testing, and real customer feedback.



WHAT YOU'LL DO


OWN CUSTOMER DEPLOYMENTS END-TO-END

 - Lead delivery of net-new AI agent deployments as the single accountable owner—from customer requirements through production go-live

 - Treat every deployment as a product launch: define scope, manage tradeoffs, hit timelines, ensure quality

 - Own the customer-facing roadmap for each deployment—what are we shipping, in what order, and why


BUILD AGENTS, NOT JUST SPECS

 - Gather requirements directly from customers and translate them into agent behavior yourself using our internal platform—prompts, tools, guardrails, multi-agent workflows

 - Do hands-on prompt engineering using AI tools to build the components that make agents work: conversation flows, decision logic, edge case handling, integration behaviors

 - Ship production-ready agents that handle real healthcare complexity: scheduling logic, patient context, EMR data, practice-specific edge cases

 - Continuously improve agent performance using structured evals, live call review, and direct customer feedback


DRIVE ZERO-TO-ONE EXECUTION

 - Lead deployments for new workflows, integrations, and verticals where no playbook exists

 - Identify unknowns early, derisk them, and make tradeoff decisions that keep launches on track

 - When deployments require net-new platform capabilities, own requirements and prioritization with the engineering team


WORK ALONGSIDE CONVERSATIONAL DESIGN AND CUSTOMER ENGINEERING

 - Conversational designers design and optimize agent behavior—they review real conversations, identify patterns, design conversation flows, and ship agent behavior

 - Customer engineers build integrations into our customers' systems of record—EHRs, PMS, and PIMS

 - You own everything else: integration scoping with Customer Engineering, cross-team coordination, timeline management, stakeholder communication, and unblocking whatever's in the way


BUILD TOWARD REPEATABILITY

 - Identify patterns across launches and codify them into playbooks

 - Know when a workflow or integration is ready to transition from zero-to-one ownership to steady-state implementation

 - Hand off proven patterns to implementation teams and move on to the next net-new challenge


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST HAVE

 - 3+ years in Product Management or a closely related role (e.g., technical program management at a product-led company, founder/early employee wearing the PM hat) or a technical background (software engineer / forward-deployed engineer) who has operated in a PM-like capacity

 - Strong technical product background—comfortable with APIs, integrations, and understanding how systems connec

 - Strong technical product background—comfortable with APIs, integrations, and understanding how systems connec

source_jobs.title: Head of Product
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hello Patient is looking for a Head of Product to define and scale how product decisions are made across the company. As our product surface area continues to expand, you'll serve as the bridge among customers, engineering, operations, and executive leadership, building strong trust across teams and ensuring we build the right products in the right order for the right reasons.

You'll own product strategy, roadmap prioritization, customer discovery, and product execution across multiple teams. We're looking for a senior IC product leader with exceptional product sense, strong execution, and deep curiosity about customer problems. You'll work directly with customers and internal teams, uncovering opportunities, writing PRDs, shaping product strategy, partnering closely with engineering & operations, and leveraging modern AI tools to accelerate product development.

Working directly with the CEO, engineering, and operations leadership, you'll bring clarity to complex decisions, create alignment across teams, and help establish the systems and processes that enable us to scale not just our product but also our internal teams, so each person at Hello Patient becomes more high-leverage with AI workflows.

The ideal candidate is equally comfortable debating strategy, diving into workflow details with customers, and rolling up their sleeves to turn ideas into shipped products. This is a senior individual contributor role focused on owning product strategy and outcomes while helping build the foundation of the product function at Hello Patient. Although the scope may evolve over time, this role does not include people management responsibilities initially. When appropriate, we expect you to build and ship products yourself, not just direct the engineering teams.



WHAT YOU'LL DO


PRODUCT STRATEGY & ROADMAP

 - Own and prioritize product roadmaps across multiple product areas and engineering pods.

 - Balance competing stakeholder requests while maintaining a holistic view of company priorities and long-term product direction.

 - Drive product decisions through data, customer insights, business impact, and strong product judgment.


CUSTOMER DISCOVERY & PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT

 - Lead customer discovery efforts, including regular onsite visits and customer feedback.

 - Partner closely with customers to uncover workflow opportunities and identify high-impact product investments.

 - Translate customer feedback and business requirements into clear product specifications and PRDs that engineering teams can execute on.


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

 - Act as the primary bridge between executive leadership, engineering, and product stakeholders.

 - Partner with engineering leads to ensure clarity on priorities, tradeoffs, and success metrics.

 - Build and improve product processes that enable the company to scale efficiently.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST HAVE

 - 5+ years of product management experience as an IC, ideally in B2B SaaS or healthcare technology.

 - Strong technical background; Computer Science degree, software engineering, or equivalent technical experience required

 - Experience building AI, LLM-based products, automation, agent-based systems, or workflow software.

 - Based in Austin, TX or New York, NY, or willing to relocate.

 - Proven experience owning product strategy and roadmap decisions for complex software products

 - Strong customer discovery skills with a track record of translating customer insights into successful products.

 - Experience writing high-quality PRDs and driving products from concept through launch.

 - Demonstrated ability to influence and challenge stakeholders through thoughtful, data-driven debate.

 - Experience working closely with engineering teams in highly technical environments.

 - Familiarity with AI-powered products, workflows, or developer tooling.

 - High agency, strong ownership mentality, and willingness to operate as a hands-on individual contributor.

notes: High volume of product and engineering roles, with a focus on AI and healthcare. Multiple duplicate postings.
